long-title: We know more about the health of some groups of people than others.
icon: bar chart, diagram, data, analysis, comparison.svg

content: People who are at risk or outside everyday society (e.g. homeless people)
victims of domestic violence
People in care or who have left care
People who have been in the armed forces
People with dementia
People with learning or development disabilities
Charities
People who receive or give unpaid care
People who are refugees
People who are seeking asylum
People who are in the country illegally
People who look after health and social care data
People who do not turn up for appointments
The general public

sections.long-title: What are the causes of the problem?
content: We don't always get information about people who:
don't have enough money
don't feel part of society
can't speak English
are receiving or giving unfunded care
are a refugee
find it hard to get care
have learning or development needs
do not have a home address
People and teams who provide services working together
Organisations having good policies and systems in place
People who provide care being friendly and welcoming
Having services that people can use
Developing health information in other languages
Working with people who teach English
Having specialist trusted link workers

sections.long-title: What actions are involved in doing something about this?
content: Mapping out the teams and organisations that provide services
Working with people who provide services
Working with people who receive services
Setting goals
Telling people what they are entitled to
Helping people claim what they are entitled to
Using information we already have
Building trust
Teaching people how to use the systems
Supporting people to become more active in their own health

sections.long-title: How will we deliver these actions?
content: Develop better datasets
Develop data systems that talk to each other
Develop a central database
Improve systems

sections.long-title: What do we expect to come out of these actions?
content: More people will be included in datasets
Clear use of language and terms across systems

sections.long-title: How will we know things have changed?
content: Higher number (and proportion) of previously excluded people on databases
Being able to measure health of more people (EQ5D)
Being able to describe levels of illness in more people
Being able to measure wellbeing of more people (Edinburgh wellbeing scale)

sections.long-title: What short term changes could we see?
content: We will know more about what different populations need
We will have action plans to deliver better services
We will know how we are going to monitor and evaluate action plans
Systems will work better for people

sections.long-title: What do we think will happen in the longer term (3 years)? For example, increased health literacy, better disease control, improved health
content: Better health
Less illness
Systems that are easier to use
Systems that work better together
Less health inequality

sections.long-title: What are the current activities in this area?
